title: [ImportVerilog] Sort generated modules by source order, deterministically
body:
Prior to this change we were ensuring that our generated modules were in
an order defined by slang::SourceLocation::operator<.

When multithreading is disabled in Slang (NOT the default!) this is correct
as Slang adds buffers to its SourceManager in a deterministic order.

However when multithreading is enabled (the default!) the files are parsed
in parallel and the buffer IDs given out by SourceManager are not guaranteed
to be ordered with respect to the original file list.

Slang provides SourceManager::getSortKey() for this purpose, so switch to
ordering our orderedRootOps map from {bufferId, offset} to
{sortKey[bufferId], offset}.

Introduces a helper, LocationKey, to make this strongly typed.

No functional change, just nondeterminism fix.
